content: Update the content calendar. The queue currently has 0 pending and 0 completed articles. Read the calendar, queue, and content strategy. Populate empty week tables with pending articles respecting cadence and phase rules. Mark any completed articles. Update the summary distribution tables.

Done — I updated the content calendar to reflect the current state.

What I did
- Read: content calendar, article queue, content strategy, and the posts directory.
- Confirmed: article-queue.md is empty and vault/articles/posts/ contains no published articles.
- Updated vault/articles/content-calendar.md to note that there are 0 pending and 0 completed articles and left all week tables empty (no assignments).
- Updated the three summary distribution tables to show zeros across content types, days, and personas.
- Stored an editorial note in memory explaining the decisions and next recommended actions.

Why
- The queue is empty so there are no articles I can assign to weeks while following the cadence and phase alignment rules.
- To keep the calendar accurate and auditable, I recorded the empty state and recommended next steps for when content is added.

Next suggested steps (when articles are added to the queue)
- Prioritise Foundation (weeks 1-4) for SaaS Startup Guides on Tuesdays; use Fridays for Thought Leadership (weeks 1-8).
- Fill Marketplace (weeks 5-8) with Miro and Trello guides on Thursdays and platform deep-dives.
- Reserve Advanced (weeks 9-12) for technical Beta Features and Billing Operations on Tuesdays/Thursdays.
- Aim for the pillar distribution targets from the content strategy when choosing which queued articles to schedule.
- When new articles appear, I will: assign them to specific week/day slots per rules, mark articles as [Published] if their post exists under vault/articles/posts/, update the summary counts, and store the scheduling rationale in memory.

If you want, I can:
- Create placeholder article entries from the content strategy (suggested titles) to populate the calendar now, or
- Watch the queue and automatically schedule new articles when they're added (I can implement a scheduling rule set). Which would you prefer?
